rules.name: readme-read-before-edit
rules.evidence: Before editing or creating files in any subdirectory (e.g., `packages/*`, `crates/*`), read all `README.md` files in the directory path from the repo root up to and including the target file's directory.

rules.name: readme-read-before-write
rules.evidence: Before editing or creating files in any subdirectory (e.g., `packages/*`, `crates/*`), read all `README.md` files in the directory path from the repo root up to and including the target file's directory.

rules.name: dev-watch-before-source-edit
rules.evidence: If you are changing Next.js source or integration tests, start `pnpm --filter=next dev` in a separate terminal session before making edits (unless it is already running). If you skip this, explicitly state why (for example: docs-only, read-only investigation, or CI-only analysis).

rules.name: bootstrap-build-after-branch-switch
rules.evidence: Always run a full bootstrap build after switching branches:

rules.name: no-no-turbopack-flag
rules.evidence: There is no `--no-turbopack` flag.

rules.name: mandatory-new-test-generation
rules.evidence: Generating tests using `pnpm new-test` is mandatory.

rules.name: no-settimeout-in-tests
rules.evidence: Use `retry()` from `next-test-utils` instead of `setTimeout` for waiting

rules.name: no-check-deprecated
rules.evidence: Do NOT use `check()` - it is deprecated. Use `retry()` + `expect()` instead

rules.name: prefer-fixture-directories
rules.evidence: Prefer real fixture directories over inline `files` objects

rules.name: pr-description-llm-comment
rules.evidence: When writing PR descriptions, you MUST include the following HTML comment at the bottom of the description:

rules.name: no-secret-files-commit
rules.evidence: Never commit local secret files; if documenting env setup, use placeholder-only examples.

rules.name: no-https-remote-switch
rules.evidence: Do not switch remotes to HTTPS, mutate remote URLs, retry repeatedly, or attempt another authentication workaround unless the user explicitly requests it.

rules.name: ssh-preflight-before-force-push
rules.evidence: Before a force-push or stack rebase that may hydrate partial-clone objects, prefer a lightweight SSH preflight.

rules.name: no-ai-footer-in-commit
rules.evidence: Do NOT add "Generated with Claude Code" or co-author footers to commits or PRs

rules.name: commit-message-style
rules.evidence: Keep commit messages concise and descriptive

rules.name: pre-validate-before-commit
rules.evidence: Pre-validate before committing to avoid slow lint-staged failures (~2 min each)

rules.name: pr-description-focus
rules.evidence: PR descriptions should focus on what changed and why

rules.name: no-pr-ready
rules.evidence: Do NOT mark PRs as "ready for review" (`gh pr ready`) - leave PRs in draft mode and let the user decide when to mark them ready

rules.name: rebuild-before-integration-tests
rules.evidence: When running Next.js integration tests, you must rebuild if source files have changed:

rules.name: flags-config-shared-wiring
rules.evidence: New flags: add type in `config-shared.ts`, schema in `config-schema.ts`, and `define-env.ts` when used in user-bundled code.

rules.name: edge-define-env-flags
rules.evidence: In edge builds, force feature flags that gate Node-only imports to `false` in `define-env.ts`.

rules.name: react-server-dom-webpack-import-scope
rules.evidence: `react-server-dom-webpack/*` imports must stay in `entry-base.ts`; consume via component module exports elsewhere.

rules.name: app-page-template-require-constraint
rules.evidence: **`app-page.ts` is a build template compiled by the user's bundler**: Any `require()` in this file is traced by webpack/turbopack at `next build` time. You cannot require internal modules with relative paths because they won't be resolvable from the user's project.

rules.name: router-act-no-browser-back
rules.evidence: Never use `browser.back()` to return to a page where accordion links are already visible — BFCache restores state and triggers uncontrolled re-prefetches.

rules.name: rust-avoid-super-imports
rules.evidence: Avoid adding new `super::` imports except in inline `mod` blocks (e.g. `mod tests { ... }`) — prefer `crate::`-rooted paths.

rules.name: docs-highlight-line-count
rules.evidence: When adding `highlight={...}` attributes to code blocks, carefully count the actual line numbers within the code block

rules.name: security-review-nonstandard-headers
rules.evidence: When reviewing PRs: if new code reads a request header that is not a standard HTTP header (like `content-type`, `accept`, `user-agent`, `host`, `authorization`, `cookie`, etc.), flag it for security review.

uncompilable: Build Commands / Testing / Linting command reference sections — plain documentation of available commands, no action to bind
Never re-run the same test suite with different grep filters; capture output once — a cross-call behavioral pattern, not a single tool-call trigger
Run the pr-status script when the user asks about CI failures/PR status — triggered by user intent, not a specific tool call
General PR-status triage rules (prioritize blocking failures, assume failures real, reproduce with CI env vars, mode-specific test command) — broad judgment guidance
CI Analysis Tips conditional fix suggestions per failure type (cargo fmt, prettier --write) — reactive to CI output, not a proactive tool-call trigger
Key Directories / Development Tips reference listings — informational, no action
NODE_ENV vs __NEXT_DEV_SERVER semantic explanation — informational, not a proscribed action
Never print or paste secret values in chat responses/commits/logs — too broad to bind to one tool call without false positives
Mirror CI env names/modes but don't inline literal secret values — no regex-detectable trigger
If a required secret is missing locally, stop and ask the user — conditional on runtime state, not a tool-call trigger
When sharing command output, summarize and redact sensitive-looking values — broad, no single trigger
Specialized Skills list ($pr-status-triage, $flags, etc.) — routing reference, not a rule
Context-Efficient Workflows section (grep first, don't re-read, batch edits, save API responses) — general efficiency guidance, mostly Read-tool based which is outside Bash|Edit|Write
Task Decomposition and Verification bullets (split work, verify each step, ask when unclear) — broad process guidance, not tied to one action
define-env.ts affects user bundling vs runtime internals — explanatory semantics, not a prohibition/requirement
Keep require() behind compile-time if/else for DCE (avoid early-return/throw) — require() is too ubiquitous to scope narrowly without heavy false positives
If a flag is consumed in pre-compiled runtime internals, wire next-server.ts/export/worker.ts — too conditional/broad to scope as a file-edit trigger

Test Gotchas informational tips (cache components/PPR default behavior, quick smoke testing with toy apps, mode-specific skipStart, log message matching, snapshot env-flag sensitivity, CI repro env vars, __NEXT_SHOW_IGNORE_LISTED stack traces) — troubleshooting/how-to knowledge, not proactive action triggers
cargo fmt ASCII ordering tip and ICE incremental-artifact troubleshooting — informational/conditional troubleshooting, not a proactive trigger
Node.js Source Maps debugging tips (--enable-source-maps, path formats, process.cwd() differences) — informational debugging knowledge
Stale Native Binary troubleshooting (delete packages/next-swc/native/*.node) — conditional troubleshooting, not a proactive trigger
